title: Hosted Deployment Options and Split-Cloud Architecture Exploration
description: Evaluate hosted deployment patterns for Enki, including single-cloud, split-cloud, and portability-preserving hybrid options, while preserving governed authority boundaries and keeping unresolved production controls explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Single-cloud, split-cloud, and relevant hybrid or portability-preserving hosting patterns are compared through one explicit decision matrix
Split-cloud analysis covers both provider-split and control-plane/data-plane separation patterns and does not assume either is superior
Reference architectures identify compute, canonical data, identity, database isolation, key custody, secrets, network, observability, backup, disaster recovery, and support boundaries
Tradeoffs include failure domains, latency, egress, cost, operational complexity, vendor lock-in, portability, privacy, compliance, and recovery behavior
Each finalist maps its effect on unresolved Sprint 23 production prerequisites without manufacturing validation evidence
At least one single-cloud and one split-cloud architecture have explicit threat, trust-boundary, failure-domain, and data-flow analyses
A cost model, migration and rollback considerations, shortlist, and human decision criteria are independently reviewable
No production infrastructure is provisioned and no hosting option is represented as production-approved by completion of this exploration
